Authority + Familiarity + Conformity.definition: The tendency to trust instructions from a perceived authority, particularly when the message references a familiar system or context and suggests that many peers have already complied.
Authority + Familiarity + Conformity.phishing_context: Message impersonates an authority figure while referencing a familiar organizational system (e.g., HR portal or company login) and implying that most colleagues have already completed the requested action.
examples: HR Department: The employee portal you normally use has a policy update—most staff members have already signed in to confirm their acknowledgment.
IT Security Team: Your regular company login page has been updated. The majority of employees have already verified their accounts—please complete yours now.
From Executive Administration: The shared document platform used by your team has been updated, and most team members have already confirmed access.

Urgency + Scarcity + Anchoring.definition: The tendency to prioritize immediate action when a message emphasizes a limited opportunity while highlighting a specific numerical detail that anchors attention.
Urgency + Scarcity + Anchoring.phishing_context: Message presents a precise number (e.g., amount, quota, or usage level) to focus attention, claims limited availability, and urges immediate action before the opportunity expires.
examples: Your account has reached 1,985 MB of 2,000 MB storage—only a few upgrade slots remain today. Upgrade within the next 15 minutes to avoid service interruption.
Invoice #41782 for $63.12 requires confirmation. Only a limited number of same-day approvals are accepted—please review immediately.
Your benefits adjustment of $52.40 is pending—only a few verification windows remain today. Confirm now to finalize processing.

Urgency + Scarcity + Authority.definition: The tendency to respond quickly when a perceived authority communicates that a limited opportunity or resource requires immediate action.
Urgency + Scarcity + Authority.phishing_context: Message impersonates an authority figure who claims that only a small number of opportunities remain and that immediate action is required.
examples: IT Security Notice: Only a small number of employees remain unverified. Complete your account confirmation immediately to avoid automatic restriction.
From Executive Finance: Only a few approval slots remain for today’s payment batch—please review and authorize within the next 20 minutes.
HR Director Alert: Limited enrollment spots remain for the benefits update. Complete the required confirmation immediately.

Overconfidence + Conformity + Anchoring.definition: The tendency to overestimate one’s judgment while being influenced by a specific reference detail and the perceived behavior of others.
Overconfidence + Conformity + Anchoring.phishing_context: Message highlights a precise number or identifier to anchor attention, suggests that many peers have already acted, and implies the recipient should easily verify it due to their experience.
examples: Invoice #49217 for $61 has already been reviewed by several team members—given your experience, you should be able to quickly confirm it as well.
Security alert ID 882041 has been validated by most administrators—your expertise should make confirming it straightforward.
Document reference #7312 has already been approved by colleagues—please take a quick look and finalize your confirmation.

Overconfidence + Familiarity + Urgency.definition: The tendency to trust familiar sources while overestimating one’s ability to quickly judge legitimacy, combined with pressure to act immediately.
Overconfidence + Familiarity + Urgency.phishing_context: Message references a familiar system or service, flatters the recipient’s expertise, and pressures them to complete a quick verification or update immediately.
examples: Your usual payroll portal needs a quick confirmation—given your familiarity with the system, you can complete this update in seconds.
Security check for your regular company login: Experienced users like you can quickly verify this session—please confirm immediately.
Your standard HR portal requires a brief verification today—since you already know the process, you can finish this update right away.
